title: Fossil Fuel Producing Economies Have Greater Potential for Interfuel Substitution

summary.about: Why countries with fossil-fuel production may switch among energy sources more easily.
summary.audience: Energy and climate policymakers, modelers and resource economists.
summary.method: Extends interfuel-substitution analysis to include transaction costs, technological adjustment and production potential, then runs policy simulations.
summary.insights: Fossil-fuel producers show higher substitution elasticities, potentially lowering the cost of climate and energy policies.
